title: NOC 22214 – Technical Occupations in Geomatics and Meteorology | GTR Immigration
description: Technical professionals in Geomatics and Meteorology are in strong demand across Canada, fueled by climate-monitoring programs, infrastructure mapping, natural-resource development, satellite imaging, and disaster-risk management initiatives. Classified under NOC 22214 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Professionals in this occupation typically work across several specializations:
Operate airborne cameras, radar & laser sensors
Develop custom GIS tools & web systems
employment_requirements: College diploma in geomatics, GIS, cartography, photogrammetry, or remote sensing
Meteorological technicians must complete federal training programs
Specialized post-hire training for some roles
In Quebec, regulatory-body membership required for the title Professional Technologist
job_outlook: This competitive outlook still favours candidates with:
additional_information: Canadian employers are actively hiring for roles in:

Climate-monitoring agenciesSatellite-imaging & mapping firmsForestry & mining operationsTransportation & utilities companiesEngineering & consulting firmsFederal & provincial departmentsMedia & environmental-forecasting services
Annual salaries typically range between $38,500–$92,600 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and certification.
